mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50 percent of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts yields positive forward returns in over 55 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals stronger near-term corporate cash flows and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment produces negative forward returns in over 50 percent of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profitability, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns with a p-value greater than 0.10 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production greater than 0.5 percent signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruptions, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months where industrial production falls by more than 0.5 percent.
